title: Economics telemetry ingest request
description: One idempotent batch of economics telemetry events (funnel, run economics, receipts) submitted to POST /api/v1/economics/telemetry. The batch names the contract and its version explicitly, so an ingestion service never has to guess the shape it received, and carries an idempotency key so a retried batch is replayed rather than double-counted — double-counting a funnel is indistinguishable from uptake, which is the one thing this telemetry exists to measure.

batch_id.description: Emitter-generated id for this batch, echoed in the response.

idempotency_key.description: Idempotency key for the batch. Also accepted via the Idempotency-Key header, matching the Planner V2 convention; when both are present they must agree (backend obligation). Re-submitting the same key must replay the original result.

events.description: The events in this batch. Bounded at 500 so a single request cannot be used as an unbounded-work lever; larger emissions page through multiple idempotent batches.

x-backend-obligations: IDEMPOTENT PERSISTENCE: the same idempotency_key must replay the first result and never re-apply the batch. The same event_id inside a later batch is a duplicate, counted as duplicate, never stored twice.
IMMUTABILITY: stored events are append-only. A differing body under an already-seen event_id/idempotency_key is a conflict, not an update.

TENANT OWNERSHIP: project_ref/run_id/advice_id in every event must be resolved against the canonical authenticated request context — never from a client-supplied X-Tenant-Id header or body field. A reference belonging to another tenant is rejected (tenant_scope_violation), not stored.
FUNNEL ORDER: stage sequencing per project/advice cannot be checked by a single event; the backend reconciles order and reports funnel_order_violation.
PROPOSER != VERIFIER: receipt attestation independence must be enforced at ingestion.
HEADER/BODY AGREEMENT: when the Idempotency-Key header and body idempotency_key are both present they must match.

SHARING POLICY: the client is the enforcement point for field-level sharing. The backend must not infer or reconstruct a withheld value from other fields; a withheld field is absent by design, not missing data to be filled in.
x-traigent-note: Contract-first per the workspace cross-repo rule: this schema lands before backend ingestion and SDK emission. It is registered under the planned (non-canonical) contract root and is NOT asserted against backend parity — no backend route serves it yet.
